output:
# GIMBAL: A Per-Device Joint Noise-and-Power Allocation Field for Secure and Private Over-the-Air Federated Learning

Here is a high-level summarized insight of a research field Machine Learning.

Here are the five core questions:

## 1. Background and Current State of the Art

Federated learning (FL) keeps raw data on-device but pays a per-round uplink cost that grows with the number of devices [1]. Over-the-air FL (OTA-FL) removes that bottleneck by exploiting the analog superposition property of the wireless multiple-access channel: devices transmit amplitude-aligned updates simultaneously on a shared resource, and the channel itself computes the sum the aggregator needs, so aggregation bandwidth becomes essentially INDEPENDENT of the device count [2], [3], [4], [20]. This has hardened into a deployable recipe -- analog gradient descent over fading multiple-access channels [5], over-the-air FedAvg from heterogeneous data (COTAF) [6], and channel-inversion / power-control transmit rules that keep the superposed sum unbiased [7], [8].

The same physics that makes aggregation free makes the update PUBLIC at the physical layer: an honest-but-curious server observes a function of every device's signal, and any external receiver in range observes a DIFFERENT linear combination through its own channel -- exactly the wiretap setting [21]. Because there is no envelope to encrypt around an analog superposition, protection must be injected INTO the transmitted waveform as calibrated noise: artificial noise for secrecy [22], and differential-privacy noise for membership/gradient protection [14], [23], [24]. The source's Yan et al. [10] line (reference 78 in the source survey) formalizes exactly this regime for OTA-FL: a designated SUBSET of devices emits Gaussian artificial noise to degrade the eavesdropper SNR; privacy and security are scored by differential privacy (DP) and MSE-security; a channel-weighted post-processing step de-biases the superposed sum; and a low-complexity branch-and-bound algorithm schedules who transmits. A parallel pairwise-suppressible artificial-noise line builds noise that cancels at the aggregator but obfuscates outsiders -- in effect integrating DP and secure aggregation at the physical layer [16], [13], [22].

The survey's own open challenges name the two unresolved axes this work targets: Challenge C (data heterogeneity / the NEAR-FAR effect -- small-data, low-power, far devices are submerged in receiver noise and dropped from the sum [7], [8], [9]) and Challenge D (secure and trustworthy OTA-FL). These are addressed today by five communities that publish largely separately: (i) privacy-preserving / DP-OTA-FL [10], [11], [12], [15]; (ii) OTA power control and device scheduling for near-far inclusion [2], [7], [8], [9]; (iii) per-client / individualized DP-FL at the algorithm layer [14], [17], [25]; (iv) secure aggregation, cryptographic or via pairwise physical masks [16], [13]; and (v) fairness-in-FL [18], [19]. Each optimizes one side of the problem.

The confound this proposal names is WATERLINE. Deployed secure OTA-FL controls privacy/security through ONE global artificial-noise/transmit-power budget N, which is simultaneously forced to be a per-device privacy FLOOR (protection requires N >= F_k, with F_k decreasing in the device's privacy budget epsilon_k) and a per-device near-far inclusion CEILING (survival requires N <= C_k, with C_k increasing in channel gain |h_k| and data importance I_k). A single scalar is feasible only if max_k F_k <= min_k C_k; under heterogeneity -- a privacy-demanding device with large F_k, a fragile small-data/far device with small C_k -- the intersection is EMPTY: a representational impossibility, not a tuning failure. Worse, the failure is SILENT, because pooled MSE and mean accuracy are dominated by strong, near, large-data devices, so the drowned, under-protected tail never registers. GIMBAL replaces the scalar N with a per-device JOINT (artificial-noise, transmit-power) allocation FIELD conditioned on (epsilon_k, I_k, h_k), with two orthogonal knobs -- SHROUD (aggregator-nullspace / pairwise-suppressible noise; privacy) and LIFT (importance-and-channel-aware power; inclusion) -- that exact-collapses to Yan et al. [10] when the population is homogeneous.

## 2. Key Findings and Analysis

The analysis is organized around the five core research questions. Q1 states the problem; Q2 (importance), Q3 (difficulty), Q4 (novelty / why-unsolved), and Q5 (approach and results) develop it, with epistemic labels held throughout: exact-collapse and boundary reductions are [provable-by-construction]; tail/privacy/fairness improvements are [conjectured->measured]; no guaranteed improvement is ever asserted.

### [Question 1] - What is the problem?

In secure and private over-the-air federated learning, can replacing the single global artificial-noise/transmit-power budget -- which the source's near-far challenge (C) and trustworthiness challenge (D) couple through one scalar -- with GIMBAL, a per-device joint artificial-noise (SHROUD, aggregator-nullspace/pairwise-suppressible) and transmit-power (LIFT, importance-and-channel-aware) allocation field conditioned on each device's privacy budget epsilon_k, data-importance I_k, and channel h_k, reproduce Yan et al.'s uniform-noise scheme allocation-for-allocation at the homogeneous identity default (E_collapse <= 1e-6) while reducing near-far model loss and per-device privacy and fairness disparity under heterogeneity, with no change to the learning model and reusing only estimated CSI?

### [Question 2] - Why is it interesting and important?

P1 (OTA-FL is the SCALABILITY play, which is exactly what makes physical-layer privacy/security mandatory, not optional). Federated learning removed raw data from the wire but left a per-round communication bottleneck that grows with the number of devices [1]. Over-the-air FL (OTA-FL) removes that bottleneck by exploiting the analog superposition property of the wireless multiple-access channel: all devices transmit their (amplitude-aligned) updates simultaneously on the same resource, and the channel itself computes the sum the aggregator needs, so aggregation bandwidth becomes essentially INDEPENDENT of the device count [2], [3], [4], [20]. This is the central scalability argument for wireless-edge FL and it has been hardened into a deployable recipe -- analog gradient descent over fading MACs [5], over-the-air FedAvg from heterogeneous data (COTAF) [6], and channel-inversion / power-control transmit rules that make the superposed sum unbiased [7], [8]. But the same physics that makes aggregation free makes the update PUBLIC at the physical layer: the server observes a function of every device's signal (untrusted-aggregator exposure), and any external receiver in range observes a DIFFERENT linear combination through its own channel (eavesdropper exposure) -- exactly the wiretap setting [21]. Because there is no envelope to encrypt around an analog superposition, protection has to be injected INTO the transmitted waveform as calibrated noise -- artificial noise for secrecy [22], differential-privacy noise for membership/gradient protection [14], [23], [24] -- and that is precisely the regime the source's Yan et al. [10] line formalizes (subset-Gaussian artificial noise + DP + MSE-security + channel-weighted post-processing). So privacy/security at the physical layer is not a bolt-on to OTA-FL; it is a first-class design axis that shares the SAME power budget as aggregation. That shared budget is where WATERLINE lives.

P2 (the harm is SILENT and COMPOUNDING -- the near-far effect and the privacy cost both fall on the SAME already-disadvantaged sub-population). The near-far effect is intrinsic to analog superposition: a device's contribution survives only if its received power clears the residual-noise floor at the aggregator, so small-data, low-power, and far (low-|h_k|) devices are SILENTLY submerged and dropped from the sum [7], [8], [9]. Two facts make this consequential rather than cosmetic. First, the dropped devices are exactly the SCARCEST-data ones, whose samples are the most valuable-per-sample for a model trained on heterogeneous, non-IID data [6]; losing them is simultaneously a data-EFFICIENCY loss and a participation-EQUITY failure, yet it is invisible to pooled MSE / mean accuracy, which is dominated by the strong/near/large-data devices that arrive with the highest received power and the largest aggregation weight. Second, the privacy axis lands on the SAME victims. Uniform-epsilon DP forces every device to absorb the protection cost calibrated for the most-sensitive device, while channel-inversion power dominance means the weak device already sits closest to the noise floor [11], [12], [15]; so the device that is most likely to be near-far-excluded is also the one for which a fixed global noise level is most likely to be both an under-protection (its true epsilon_k demand unmet) and an over-burden (the shared noise it must survive). WATERLINE thus predicts a COMPOUNDING fairness harm -- exclusion and privacy-cost concentrated on one tail -- that no pooled number reports. Detecting it requires the stratified instruments the brief names: tail-device (small-data/far) accuracy, per-device epsilon-violation / MSE-security rate, and a near-far inclusion rate. The importance of the idea is partly that it makes this silent harm MEASURABLE by giving the system a per-device axis on which the harm can even be expressed.

P3 (WATERLINE reframes a pile of point-fixes as approximations to ONE missing capability). The literature already contains the individual ingredients as separate point-fixes: channel-weighted post-processing to de-bias the superposed sum after the fact [10]; device scheduling / branch-and-bound selection to decide who transmits [9], [10]; optimized power control to lift received SNR [7], [8]; pairwise-suppressible / masked secrecy structure to hide the sum from outsiders [16], [22]. Each is a correction applied at a DIFFERENT stage to a DIFFERENT symptom, and each is, in effect, an approximation to a single capability the deployed system lacks: a measured, per-device, TWO-AXIS (artificial-noise AND transmit-power) allocation FIELD that is set jointly from each device's (epsilon_k, I_k, h_k). Post-processing can only repair the MEAN distortion AFTER aggregation -- it cannot reconstruct a per-device privacy-vs-inclusion choice that was already collapsed into one scalar N at transmit time. Naming the confound WATERLINE is the contribution-shaped move: it says these are not five independent knobs to tune but five shadows of one missing degree of freedom, and it specifies the do-no-harm contract any replacement must honor (exact collapse to Yan et al. [10] when the population is homogeneous). That reframing is valuable independent of any performance number, because it converts "tune the budget better" into "add the axis the budget never had."

P4 (the pattern transfers, and the importance does NOT depend on a guaranteed win). The load-bearing trick -- put the privacy noise in the AGGREGATOR NULLSPACE so it cancels in the computed sum (sum_k h_k a_k = 0) while still degrading every other receiver (sum_k w_k a_k != 0) -- is a general principle for ANY analog-superposition / over-the-air computation setting where a trusted linear functional must be preserved while untrusted observers are degraded [20], [13]. The same "verify the privacy FLOOR and the inclusion CEILING JOINTLY, per device, not the pooled mean" discipline applies to over-the-air secure aggregation, distributed estimation, and multi-access sensing far beyond this one FL scheme. Crucially, the importance claim is epistemically modest by design: it does NOT rest on GIMBAL beating Yan et al. on accuracy (that is [conjectured->measured], conditional on heterogeneity). It rests on giving the field a measurable per-device degree of freedom it provably lacked -- a representational capability whose absence is a theorem (empty-intersection feasibility), and whose addition is do-no-harm by construction (exact collapse, budget-conserving). A direction that can only help or exactly tie a named secure baseline, while making a previously-silent tail harm instrumentable, is worth pursuing whether or not the average metric moves.

### [Question 3] - Why is it hard?

The naive design is: "give every device its OWN artificial noise a_k and its OWN transmit power p_k, and tune each independently to taste." This is not a small simplification -- it silently fails on seven concrete, INDEPENDENT counts. The difficulty is real and structural, not incidental.

**O1 -- BINDING OBSTACLE: the joint per-device noise+power allocation is NON-CONVEX, with TWO OPPOSING ATTRACTORS fighting over one budget.**

The naive failure. "Tune a_k and p_k independently" assumes the privacy subproblem and the inclusion subproblem are separable. They are not. In a generic OTA scheme the artificial noise a_k enters BOTH received signals:
```
Aggregator:   y   = sum_k h_k p_k s_k + sum_k h_k a_k + n
Eavesdropper: y_e = sum_k w_k p_k s_k + sum_k w_k a_k + n_e
```
You cannot raise Eve's noise (sum_k w_k a_k) without also raising the aggregator's noise (sum_k h_k a_k). Likewise you cannot boost a weak device's power p_k to rescue it at the aggregator without also boosting Eve's received SIGNAL w_k p_k s_k. So the two objectives are coupled through (i) the SHARED channel and (ii) the SHARED total budget (sum_k |a_k|^2 <= B_noise, |p_k|^2 <= P_max).

Why it is non-convex. The privacy/MSE-security level at Eve and the per-device privacy FLOOR F_k = F(epsilon_k) are RATIOS of the form |w_k|^2 |a_k|^2 / (received signal + AWGN) -- non-convex in (a, p) jointly. The near-far fairness objective (worst-device aggregate distortion, or an alpha-fair sum of per-device distortions) is a max/min or concave-utility over per-device effective SNR, itself a ratio in p_k vs residual noise. Co-optimizing per-device MSE-security constraints WITH a near-far fidelity+fairness objective under a TOTAL-budget cap is therefore a non-convex, budget-coupled program -- not separable, not tunable knob-by-knob.

The TWO OPPOSING ATTRACTORS (one restoring force each, both required, they conflict).
- Attractor A (privacy/security). Restoring force pulls artificial noise UP: more noise -> lower Eve SNR -> better DP / higher MSE-security -> raises the privacy floor coverage. A alone would drive the budget to MAXIMAL noise. But that DEEPENS near-far submersion (weak devices drown) and degrades aggregate fidelity. This couples to Attractor B = Challenge C (near-far).
- Attractor B (inclusion/fidelity). Restoring force pulls noise DOWN and power-to-weak UP: less aggregate noise + more power to small-data/far devices -> they clear the residual-noise floor -> faithful, fair aggregate. B alone would drive the budget to MINIMAL noise / maximal weak-device power. But that UNDER-PROTECTS the privacy-sensitive devices and raises Eve's received signal. This couples to Attractor A = Challenge D (trustworthiness).

Both restoring forces are MANDATORY (we need privacy AND inclusion) and they pull the budget in OPPOSITE directions. A single global scalar N must sit at one operating point that is simultaneously wrong for the privacy-sensitive tail (needs more noise) and the near-far-disadvantaged tail (needs less noise / more power). That is exactly the WATERLINE empty-intersection: feasible iff max_k F_k <= min_k C_k, EMPTY under heterogeneity. Naive single-dial tuning cannot escape it because there is no scalar that satisfies both attractors at once.

Why decoupling is non-trivial -- and what makes a tractable separation possible (only under assumptions). The aggregator-nullspace trick (SHROUD pairwise design with sum_k h_k a_k = 0) is the lever that makes the problem separable: it REMOVES a_k from the aggregator received signal, so artificial noise no longer fights inclusion at the aggregator. This converts ONE infeasible global interval into K independently-feasible per-device constraints -- SHROUD owns Attractor A, LIFT owns Attractor B, and the program becomes BLOCK-separable. BUT the separation is exact only under three assumptions: (1) PERFECT CSI (so the null is exact -- broken by O2); (2) KNOWN Eve channel (so the floor is computable -- broken by O3); (3) the second-order term (LIFT raising Eve's SIGNAL) is accounted for, not assumed zero (broken if ignored -- O4). When any assumption is relaxed, the blocks RE-COUPLE and the residual problem is non-convex again. So O1 is binding: even with the nullspace trick, the realistic allocation is a non-convex joint program, and O2-O7 are the concrete ways that joint structure bites. [provable-by-construction] that the problem is non-convex and that the nullspace makes it block-separable under (1)-(3); [conjectured->measured] that the SCA/alternating solver in Q5.1 reaches a good operating point.

**O2 -- Imperfect / estimated CSI breaks exact nullspace cancellation -> residual SHROUD leakage into the aggregate.**

SHROUD nulls using the ESTIMATED channel h_hat_k, but the true channel is h_k = h_hat_k + e_k. The pairwise cancellation then leaves a residual:
```
sum_k h_k a_k = sum_k (h_hat_k + e_k) a_k = sum_k e_k a_k != 0
```
So protective noise LEAKS into the aggregate, injecting distortion exactly where the design promised ZERO fidelity cost. First-order magnitude: if e_k is zero-mean with variance sigma_csi^2 and independent of a_k, the expected leakage power is approximately sigma_csi^2 * sum_k |a_k|^2 = sigma_csi^2 * B_noise -- it GROWS with the SHROUD budget and the CSI error, and it falls hardest on weak devices whose own signal is smallest relative to the leakage floor. The naive "just null at the aggregator" silently re-introduces the fidelity cost it claimed to avoid. We must (a) carry an analytic leakage bound into the budget, (b) measure it in the Leakage ablation (Q5.6), (c) let the do-no-harm gate and LIFT margin absorb it. [conjectured->measured] (bound it, then verify the bound holds and degradation is graceful).

**O3 -- Eavesdropper CSI is statistical / uncertain -> the privacy floor must hold in expectation or worst-case over w_k.**

Eve's instantaneous channel w_k is unknown (only its distribution / an uncertainty set is known). SHROUD's Eve-facing degradation depends on w_k:
```
w_k a_k + w_k' a_k' = c_{kk'} u (w_k/h_k - w_k'/h_k') != 0
```
Design for a NOMINAL w_k and an unlucky-but-plausible Eve realization (e.g., Eve near a device, large |w_k|) sees LESS degradation -> the per-device privacy floor F_k is violated. So the floor cannot be a point constraint; it must be a CHANCE constraint Pr_w[ MSE-security_k >= F_k ] >= 1 - delta, OR a worst-case constraint over an uncertainty set ||w_k - w_nominal|| <= gamma (robust artificial-noise design, in the lineage of Goel-Negi artificial noise [22]). The naive "design for the average Eve" silently produces a NONZERO epsilon-violation rate in the tail of w_k. [designed] (the robust/chance-constrained formulation) -> [conjectured->measured] (the realized violation rate across Eve draws).

**O4 -- Second-order coupling: LIFT power also raises Eve's received SIGNAL, partially offsetting SHROUD -- must stay INSIDE the joint optimization.**

SHROUD nulls at the aggregator, so the AGGREGATE is governed by LIFT alone (true orthogonality there). But Eve sees BOTH knobs: y_e contains sum_k w_k p_k s_k. When LIFT raises p_k to rescue a weak device, it RAISES Eve's received signal power, improving Eve's SNR and PARTIALLY UNDOING SHROUD's degradation. So at Eve, privacy is NOT a function of SHROUD alone once LIFT is active -- there is a second-order coupling term. This must be kept INSIDE the joint program: whenever LIFT boosts device k, SHROUD must add pairwise noise to hold Eve's SNR (hence F_k) at target. Assuming the term is zero would OVER-CLAIM privacy precisely for the weak devices we just boosted (the worst place to be wrong). The naive "two knobs are perfectly orthogonal, set each independently" under-protects exactly the LIFT-heavy tail. [conjectured->measured] (measure Eve SNR vs LIFT gain; show the joint solver compensates).

**O5 -- The do-no-harm homogeneity gate must not chatter near the noise floors -> hysteresis.**

The gate fires when Var_k(epsilon_k), Var_k(|h_k|), Var_k(I_k) are below floors nu_eps, nu_h, nu_I (collapse to baseline). Near a threshold, sampling noise in the per-round VARIANCE ESTIMATES makes the gate flip on/off round-to-round, so the allocation OSCILLATES between baseline and GIMBAL -- instability, and the E_collapse guarantee becomes ill-defined (is it collapsing this round or not?). Fix: HYSTERESIS -- two thresholds nu_low < nu_high (turn GIMBAL ON only above nu_high, OFF only below nu_low) plus a DWELL/debounce (state must persist M rounds before switching). A naive single-threshold gate silently chatters and intermittently breaks the do-no-harm promise. [designed] (hysteresis + dwell) -> [conjectured->measured] (chatter rate -> ~0 in the hysteresis sweep).

**O6 -- METRIC BLINDNESS: pooled accuracy / MSE cannot SEE the heterogeneous tail failure (so naive evaluation cannot even detect or attribute the effect).**

Pooled MSE and mean test accuracy are dominated by the strong (near, large-data, high-|h_k|) devices, whose updates arrive at highest received power and largest aggregation weight. The drowned small-data/far minority contributes negligibly to the pooled number, and a per-device privacy violation (N < F_k for a sensitive device) does not register in an accuracy metric AT ALL. So a naive evaluation reports SUCCESS while a sub-population is silently LOST and UNDER-PROTECTED -- you cannot DETECT or ATTRIBUTE the WATERLINE effect with pooled instruments, which makes the entire research question untestable on standard metrics. This is itself a methodology obstacle: it FORCES new STRATIFIED instruments (tail-device accuracy, worst-device accuracy, per-device epsilon-violation rate, MSE-security at Eve, near-far inclusion rate). This is the known global-metric attribution trap; we adopt that discipline. [provable-by-construction] (pooled mean is dominated by strong devices) -> [conjectured->measured] (stratified metrics reveal the gap).

**O7 -- Per-device DP accounting under analog superposition + correlated pairwise noise is subtle.**

DP for device k's update is provided by the TOTAL noise the adversary sees (its own + others' + channel AWGN). SHROUD's pairwise construction makes the noise across a pair CORRELATED (a_k = +c u/h_k, a_k' = -c u/h_k'), not independent -- so the textbook Gaussian-mechanism per-device epsilon (which assumes independent additive noise) does NOT directly apply. Correct accounting must handle (i) the correlation structure of the pairwise field, (ii) that the honest-but-curious AGGREGATOR sees a DIFFERENT effective noise (post-null) than EVE, so the two have different epsilon, (iii) composition over rounds (Renyi-DP / moments accountant). The naive "each device gets Gaussian noise of variance v, read epsilon off the standard formula" MIS-ACCOUNTS because the effective per-device noise seen by each adversary is the correlated sum, not the marginal. [designed] (per-adversary Renyi-DP accounting with the correlation kernel) -> [conjectured->measured] (accounted epsilon_k matches the empirical membership/attribute-inference attack success).

Summary of difficulty. O1 is the BINDING obstacle: a non-convex, budget-coupled joint allocation produced by two mandatory, conflicting attractors (privacy vs inclusion) over one budget; the aggregator-nullspace trick buys block-separability only under assumptions that O2 (CSI error), O3 (Eve uncertainty), and O4 (second-order coupling) each break, re-coupling the program. O5 (gate stability), O6 (metric blindness), O7 (DP accounting) are the additional concrete failure points a naive per-device design walks into.

### [Question 4] - Why hasn't it been solved before?

P1 (the recurring structural barrier: the secure budget was modeled as a SCALAR or a SCHEDULING hyperparameter, so per-device heterogeneity was literally unrepresentable). Walk the closest family first. Privacy-preserving / DP-OTA FL -- the source's Yan et al. [10], together with wireless local-DP [11], "privacy for free" via channel noise [12], and DP-FL performance analyses [14], [15] -- controls protection through an AGGREGATE artificial-noise power plus an on/off device-scheduling mask on top of a channel-inversion power rule. The privacy/security operating point is, by construction, a single global (or channel-only) scalar with a combinatorial schedule; there is no per-device continuous noise amplitude that co-varies with that device's epsilon_k AND its I_k AND its h_k. So this family can express "how much total noise and who transmits," but not "a different (noise, power) pair for each device chosen from its own three-axis context." That is barrier (a): heterogeneity was an input the representation could not hold, so the empty-intersection infeasibility (max_k F_k > min_k C_k) could not even be stated inside the model, let alone fixed. The OTA power-control and device-scheduling family [2], [7], [8], [9] is the mirror image: it DOES allocate per-device transmit power against importance and channel, but purely on the fidelity side, with no privacy/secrecy axis at all -- so it never meets the privacy FLOOR that creates the conflict.

P2 (barriers (b) and (c): privacy noise was treated as pure fidelity OVERHEAD, and the two relevant literatures never crossed). Barrier (b): in essentially every DP-OTA scheme the artificial / DP noise is a COST charged against aggregation fidelity -- more protection means a noisier sum and deeper near-far submersion [10], [11], [12], [15]. Nobody co-designed the noise so that it is FREE to the aggregate. The aggregator-nullspace / pairwise-suppressible construction (noise that cancels in the computed sum but not at the eavesdropper) is exactly the device that breaks the privacy-vs-fidelity trade-off, and it is the missing ingredient that lets the privacy FLOOR be raised at zero aggregate-fidelity cost; treating noise as unavoidable overhead removed the incentive to build it. Barrier (c): the coupled problem sits on a SEAM between four communities that publish separately. (i) OTA power control / near-far inclusion [2], [7], [8], [9] optimizes the CEILING and ignores privacy. (ii) DP-FL and individualized DP [14], [15], [17], [23], [24], [25] optimizes per-client privacy at the ALGORITHM layer (gradient-clip + Gaussian noise) and is agnostic to channel, received power, and near-far submersion -- its epsilon_k never meets a physical inclusion ceiling. (iii) Secure aggregation [16] hides the summands cryptographically (or via pairwise physical masks [13], [22]) but assumes the sum itself is reconstructed exactly -- it has no notion of a weak device being dropped, so it never couples secrecy to near-far inclusion. (iv) Fairness-in-FL [18], [19] reweights the LEARNING objective to protect under-served clients but at the algorithm layer, with no physical noise/power field and no eavesdropper. Each community optimizes one of {floor, ceiling} and treats the other as out of scope; nobody co-optimized the per-device floor AND ceiling that share one budget.

P3 (barrier (d): no exact-collapse safety bridge to a NAMED secure baseline -- and how GIMBAL differs). Even the works that add per-device structure add it UNCONDITIONALLY: individualized DP assigns each client its own epsilon_k [17], [25], and fair-FL reweights per client [18], [19], but neither reduces, by construction, to a specific deployed secure OTA-FL allocation when the population happens to be homogeneous. There was no design pressure toward a zero-gate identity, so there is no machine-checkable do-no-harm contract against a named secure baseline. GIMBAL differs on exactly the conjunction these families leave open. It makes the secure budget a per-device JOINT 2-D field (C1): for each device a PAIR (artificial-noise amplitude c_k, transmit-power gain f_lift,k), not a scalar and not a noise-only or power-only field. It conditions that field on all three heterogeneous axes at once and co-optimizes the privacy/MSE-security constraint WITH the near-far fidelity/fairness objective (C2), coupling the source's Challenge C (near-far) and Challenge D (trustworthiness) through the shared budget they actually share. It decouples the two axes ORTHOGONALLY via the aggregator nullspace (C3): SHROUD noise satisfies sum_k h_k a_k = 0 so it is invisible to the aggregate but degrades Eve (sum_k w_k a_k != 0), while LIFT governs the aggregate weighting alone -- so the one infeasible global interval becomes K independently-feasible per-device constraints, and each knob ablates without the other. And it carries an EXACT-COLLAPSE do-no-harm bridge to the named secure baseline Yan et al. [10] (C4): at the homogeneous identity (epsilon_k = epsilon, |h_k| = h, I_k = I, gates = 1) GIMBAL emits Yan et al.'s uniform subset-Gaussian + channel-weighted post-processing allocation-for-allocation, E_collapse <= 1e-6, CI-gateable and budget-conserving. The boundary cases make the unification concrete and honest: SHROUD-off recovers importance-aware OTA power control [7], [8]; LIFT-off recovers heterogeneous-DP-OTA [11]; pairwise-uniform SHROUD recovers physical secure aggregation [16], [22]; field-forced-constant recovers the single global scalar -- the deployed WATERLINE operating point GIMBAL generalizes.

P4 (the near-miss audit -- every prior family clears AT MOST 2 of C1-C4; GIMBAL is the sole occupant of the conjunction). C1 = joint per-device 2-D (noise, power) field; C2 = tri-axis conditioning (epsilon_k AND I_k AND h_k) + coupled privacy-and-near-far objective; C3 = orthogonal, independently-ablatable knobs via aggregator-nullspace decoupling; C4 = exact-collapse do-no-harm to a NAMED secure baseline (Yan et al. [10]).

| Near-miss family (refs) | C1 | C2 | C3 | C4 | count |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Privacy-preserving OTA-FL / DP-OTA -- Yan et al. [10], [11], [12], [15] | no (global noise + scheduling, not joint 2-D field) | partial (channel + security; not epsilon_k AND I_k co-opt) | no (one mechanism; no 2nd orthogonal knob; noise is overhead) | no (IS the baseline; no collapse bridge) | <= 1 |
| OTA-FL power control & device scheduling -- [2], [7], [8], [9] | no (power-only) | partial (importance + channel, fidelity side only) | no (no privacy knob) | no | 1 |
| Per-client / individualized DP-FL -- [14], [17], [25] | no (algorithmic gradient noise; no power/channel) | partial (epsilon_k only; channel/importance-agnostic) | no | no (no OTA secure-allocation baseline) | <= 1 |
| Secure aggregation (crypto / pairwise physical) -- [16], [13], [22] | no (mask/noise field; not joint with power) | partial (pairwise conditions on channel/pair, not epsilon_k AND I_k) | partial (cancellation idea; lacks the LIFT inclusion knob) | no (collapses to plain sum, not to Yan et al.) | <= 2 |
| Fairness-in-FL -- q-FFL [18], AFL [19] | no (algorithmic reweighting) | partial (importance/loss only) | no (no privacy noise knob) | no (no OTA secure baseline) | <= 1 |

The novelty is the CONJUNCTION C1 and C2 and C3 and C4: a joint, tri-axis-conditioned, aggregator-nullspace-decoupled per-device noise+power field with provable exact-collapse to a NAMED secure OTA-FL baseline. The closest family (secure aggregation) reaches 2 by sharing the cancellation intuition, but it has no inclusion/LIFT axis and no collapse to a SECURE (rather than plaintext-sum) baseline. Epistemics held exactly: the empty-intersection impossibility, the boundary reductions, the exact-collapse identity, and budget-conservation are [provable-by-construction]; the SHROUD aggregator-nullspace orthogonality is [designed] under perfect CSI and [conjectured->measured] (residual leakage) under estimated CSI; and any reduction in near-far loss or per-device privacy/fairness disparity is [conjectured->measured], conditional on heterogeneity, never asserted as a guaranteed global improvement.

### [Question 5] - What are the key components of my approach and results?

**Q5.1 -- Method pipeline (end-to-end, per communication round).** Both attractors are addressed by SEPARATE, independently-ablatable mechanisms (SHROUD for privacy, LIFT for inclusion) -- NEVER one dial. They couple only through the budget and the O4 second-order term, both kept inside the joint program.

1. CSI reuse (no new pilots). Reuse the {h_hat_k} already estimated for OTA amplitude alignment. Maintain Eve channel STATISTICS only: distribution and/or an uncertainty set for w_k (e.g., from Eve-location uncertainty or a worst-case |w_k| <= w_max). No instantaneous Eve CSI assumed (O3).
2. Per-device context phi_k = ( epsilon_k , I_k , |h_hat_k| , w_k-stats ). epsilon_k = requested privacy budget (input). I_k = data importance, estimated from local gradient norm ||g_k|| and/or local sample count n_k. |h_hat_k| = estimated aggregator gain. w_k-stats = mean / variance / worst-case bound of Eve channel.
3. SHROUD design (Attractor A; aggregator-nullspace pairwise noise). Pair devices (k, k'); set a_k = +c_{kk'} u / h_hat_k, a_k' = -c_{kk'} u / h_hat_k', so sum_k h_hat_k a_k = 0 (nulls at aggregator) while sum_k w_k a_k != 0 (degrades Eve). Choose c_{kk'} >= 0 so each device's privacy FLOOR F_k = F(epsilon_k) is met at Eve in EXPECTATION or WORST-CASE over w_k (O3). Pairing heuristic: pair a high-need (small epsilon_k) device with a high-budget partner and balance |h_k| so amplitudes u/h_k stay bounded (controls O2 leakage and the budget).
4. LIFT design (Attractor B; importance-and-channel-aware channel inversion). p_k = (sqrt(rho)/h_hat_k) * f_lift(I_k, |h_hat_k|), with f_lift >= 1 and |p_k|^2 <= P_max. f_lift boosts small-data/far/low-|h_k| devices ABOVE the residual-noise inclusion floor (the truncated-channel-inversion cutoff that drops them in the baseline). f_lift == 1 recovers plain channel inversion.
5. JOINT allocation (the non-convex program of O1).
```
minimize over {c_{kk'}}, {f_lift,k}   D_fair( effective per-device distortion )
subject to   privacy floor:   Pr_w[ MSE-security_k >= F(epsilon_k) ] >= 1 - delta   for all k   (chance / worst-case; O3)
             second-order Eve term included in MSE-security_k( c , f_lift )            (O4)
             leakage budget:  E[ |sum_k e_k a_k|^2 ] <= L_max( sigma_csi )               (O2)
             total noise:     sum_k |a_k|^2 <= B_noise                                  (budget parity)
             per-device power: |p_k|^2 <= P_max
```
Objective D_fair = worst-device aggregate distortion (max_k) OR an alpha-fair aggregate (alpha-fairness exponent; alpha=0 -> utilitarian/pooled, alpha->inf -> max-min). Solver: SUCCESSIVE CONVEX APPROXIMATION (SCA) on the non-convex ratios, wrapped in ALTERNATING optimization -- (a) fix LIFT, solve SHROUD (convexified privacy subproblem); (b) fix SHROUD, solve LIFT (fair power-allocation subproblem); iterate. A LAGRANGIAN dual handles the budget coupling. WARM-START from the previous round (channel coherence) to keep per-round cost low.
6. Channel-weighted post-processing (reuse). hat = post(y)/sqrt(rho); reuse Yan et al.'s channel-weighted post-processing / an MMSE de-bias to clean the residual AWGN n and the O2 leakage term. Yields an importance-weighted, SHROUD-free aggregate hat = sum_k f_lift(k) s_k + (residual)/sqrt(rho).
7. Do-no-harm gate with HYSTERESIS (O5). Compute Var_k over phi_k. Gate state machine: switch ON only when all variances exceed nu_high, OFF only when all fall below nu_low (< nu_high), with an M-round dwell. When OFF: c_k <- c0 (Yan et al. uniform subset-Gaussian), f_lift,k <- 1 -> EXACT baseline. When ON: GIMBAL allocation from step 5.

End-of-round: emit (c, p); aggregate; post-process; update model with the SAME FedAvg/SGD step as baseline (model untouched).

**Q5.2 -- Wireless / system model.**
- K devices (sweep K in {50, 100, 200}) at distances d_k chosen to create a realistic NEAR-FAR spread: either d_k ~ Uniform[d_min, d_max] with d_max/d_min in {3, 10, 30}, or a two-cluster cell-center vs cell-edge mix.
- Path loss PL(d_k) = (d_k / d_0)^(-beta), beta in [2, 4] (e.g., 3.5 urban). Small-scale Rayleigh fading h_k = sqrt(PL(d_k)) * g_k, g_k ~ CN(0,1). The near-far ratio is set by d_max/d_min and beta.
- AWGN at aggregator n ~ CN(0, sigma^2). Average receive-SNR regimes in {0, 10, 20} dB.
- Truncated channel inversion baseline cutoff (broadband analog aggregation, Zhu et al. [2]): devices with |h_k|^2 below a threshold are DROPPED to avoid noise amplification -- this IS the near-far submersion that LIFT counteracts.
- Eavesdropper with INDEPENDENT fading w_k = sqrt(PL(d_k^e)) * g_k^e, g_k^e ~ CN(0,1), at its own location; only STATISTICAL CSI known (O3). Eve AWGN n_e ~ CN(0, sigma_e^2).
- CSI error model h_k = h_hat_k + e_k, e_k ~ CN(0, sigma_csi^2); sweep sigma_csi for the Leakage ablation (O2).

**Q5.3 -- Datasets / FL tasks and models.**
- MNIST -- logistic regression and a small CNN / LeNet-5 (standard in Yan et al. [10] / COTAF [6] OTA-FL).
- Fashion-MNIST -- small CNN.
- CIFAR-10 -- LeNet-5 / a compact CNN, or ResNet-8 / ResNet-20.
- CIFAR-100 -- compact CNN / ResNet-20 (stresses the tail harder).
- LEAF federated-native -- FEMNIST (62-class handwritten chars, naturally non-IID by writer; 2-layer CNN) and/or Shakespeare (next-character prediction, non-IID by speaking role; stacked LSTM/GRU). These give REALISTIC quantity- and label-skew without synthetic partitioning.
- Non-IID partitioning for the image sets: Dirichlet(alpha) LABEL skew (alpha in {0.1, 0.5, 1.0}; small alpha = stronger skew, Hsu et al.) PLUS QUANTITY skew (lognormal sample counts) to manufacture small-data devices. Devices that are BOTH small-data AND far form the tail that WATERLINE drowns.

**Q5.4 -- Metrics (STRATIFIED -- make the silent failure visible; O6).**
PRIMARY (tail-facing).
- Tail-device test accuracy -- mean test accuracy over the bottom-q quantile of devices ranked by (data size, channel gain); report q = 10% and 20%.
- Worst-device accuracy -- min (or 5th-percentile) per-device test accuracy.

Privacy / security.
- Per-device DP-epsilon-violation rate -- fraction of devices whose accounted/realized epsilon_k exceeds the requested budget (O7 accounting). Target: -> ~0 for GIMBAL; NONZERO for a uniform baseline that under-protects the high-need tail.
- MSE-security at Eve -- per-device MSE of Eve's best (MMSE) estimate of s_k; higher = more secure. Report per-device and tail-stratified.

Inclusion.
- Near-far inclusion rate -- fraction of devices whose effective received update clears the residual-noise floor (effective SNR >= threshold), i.e., NOT submerged.

Parity / safety checks.
- Pooled test accuracy -- the parity check; must stay flat or improve (no-harm).
- Energy / budget parity -- assert realized totals sum_k |a_k|^2 <= B_noise AND sum_k |p_k|^2 <= baseline total power; report the numbers (proves GIMBAL only REALLOCATES).
- E_collapse = max_k || (c, p)_GIMBAL - (c, p)_baseline ||_inf at the homogeneous identity; assert <= 1e-6 (CI gate).
- Fairness dispersion -- max-minus-min and variance of per-device accuracy; convergence rounds to a target accuracy.

**Q5.5 -- Baselines (ALL at EQUAL total noise+power budget and EQUAL channel draws / seeds).**
- B1 = Yan et al. [10] secure-private OTA-FL -- subset-Gaussian artificial noise + channel-weighted post-processing + branch-and-bound device scheduling. PRIMARY baseline.
- B2 = COTAF / no-noise OTA-FL (Sery et al. [6], convergent analog aggregation with time-varying precoding; no privacy). The no-privacy fidelity ceiling.
- B3 = heterogeneous-DP-OTA, near-far-blind -- per-device DP noise from epsilon_k, NO inclusion correction. Isolates the privacy axis.
- B4 = importance-aware OTA power control, no privacy -- channel-inversion + importance weighting / truncated channel inversion (Zhu et al. [2]). Isolates the inclusion axis.
- B5 = pairwise-suppressible secure aggregation -- pairwise noise that cancels at the aggregator, UNIFORM amplitude, no LIFT (the source's pairwise-noise line).
- B6 = q-FFL-style fairness on a uniform secure budget -- q-FedAvg (Li et al. [18], ICLR 2020) reweighting ON TOP of a single global secure scalar (fairness via the model, not via the physical-layer field). Tests whether algorithmic fairness alone closes the near-far gap.

**Q5.6 -- Ablations.**
- SHROUD x LIFT 2x2 factorial (off/on each): (off,off) ~ no-privacy uniform (B2-like); (on,off) = SHROUD-only (B3 / B5); (off,on) = LIFT-only (B4); (on,on) = full GIMBAL (B1 is the uniform-SHROUD corner). Directly maps the design to boundary cases B1-B4 and proves orthogonal ablatability (C3).
- Oracle (genie): per-device floor F_k and ceiling C_k from FULL CSI INCLUDING instantaneous Eve w_k -> upper bound on achievable tail/privacy; quantifies the cost of statistical-Eve uncertainty (O3).
- Placebo: RANDOM per-device allocation with MATCHED MARGINAL budget (same total noise+power, randomly distributed, NOT conditioned on phi_k). Proves the SIGNAL (conditioning on epsilon_k, I_k, h_k) drives the gains -- not mere heterogeneity in the allocation.
- Leakage ablation (O2): sweep CSI error sigma_csi; measure residual SHROUD leakage into the aggregate and its effect on tail accuracy; check against the sigma_csi^2 * B_noise bound.
- Eve-CSI robustness sweep (O3): nominal vs chance-constrained (delta) vs worst-case (gamma) SHROUD design; measure epsilon-violation rate across Eve channel realizations.
- Second-order-coupling ablation (O4): joint program WITH vs WITHOUT the Eve-signal term; measure Eve SNR / privacy-floor violation as LIFT gain increases.
- Gate hysteresis sweep (O5): vary [nu_low, nu_high] and dwell M near the homogeneous boundary; measure allocation chatter (flip frequency) and confirm E_collapse stays <= 1e-6 in the homogeneous regime.

**Q5.7 -- Falsifiable predictions / hypotheses-with-ranges [conjectured->measured].**
- P1 (tail inclusion -- LIFT). Under heterogeneity (alpha = 0.1, near-far ratio >= 10x), at FIXED total budget, GIMBAL improves tail-device (bottom-20%) test accuracy by +8 to +20 percentage points on CIFAR-10 and +5 to +15 pp on MNIST / FEMNIST over B1. [conjectured->measured].
- P2 (privacy floor -- SHROUD). Per-device epsilon-violation rate -> ~0 for GIMBAL vs ~10-40% of high-need devices violated for uniform B1, at EQUAL total noise budget; MSE-security at Eve for the high-need tail strictly higher. [conjectured->measured].
- P3 (do-no-harm + attribution). ~0 change on POOLED accuracy (within +/-0.5 pp), EXACT collapse at the homogeneous identity (E_collapse <= 1e-6 verifier PASSES), and GIMBAL > Placebo on tail accuracy (gap attributable to the SIGNAL, not heterogeneity). [provable-by-construction] for the collapse; [conjectured->measured] for the pooled-parity and the Placebo gap.
- P4 (graceful leakage -- O2). Residual SHROUD leakage grows monotonically with sigma_csi and stays bounded by ~ sigma_csi^2 * B_noise; tail accuracy degrades GRACEFULLY (no cliff) up to a stated sigma_csi. [conjectured->measured].

Refutation triples (what result would FALSIFY the claim).
- (R1) Inclusion falsified: strong heterogeneity, fixed budget, tail-accuracy gain over B1 <= +1 pp (within noise) -> LIFT delivers no near-far inclusion; the inclusion half of the gap is wrong.
- (R2) Privacy falsified: equal total noise budget, GIMBAL's per-device epsilon-violation rate statistically indistinguishable from B1's -> SHROUD provides no per-device floor; the privacy half is wrong.
- (R3) Do-no-harm / attribution falsified: at the homogeneous identity E_collapse > 1e-6, OR Placebo matches GIMBAL on tail accuracy at matched marginal budget -> either the construction is unsafe or the gains are mere heterogeneity, not the conditioned field.

**Q5.8 -- Q1 <-> Q5 consistency check.** Q1 promises exactly two things, with calibrated epistemics: (a) [provable-by-construction] allocation-for-allocation collapse to Yan et al. [10] at the homogeneous identity (E_collapse <= 1e-6); (b) [conjectured->measured] reduced near-far model loss and per-device privacy/fairness disparity under heterogeneity -- with NO model change and CSI reuse, and deliberately NO claim of provable improvement. Q5 delivers precisely this and no more: the provable collapse (a) is verified by the E_collapse CI gate and the 2x2 factorial / boundary cases B1-B6 (all [provable-by-construction]); the measured gains (b) are P1 (tail accuracy), P2 (epsilon-violation rate + MSE-security), and the fairness-dispersion metric, each with an explicit refutation condition (R1-R3), so they are falsifiable, not asserted. No over-claim: SHROUD's exact null is [designed] under perfect CSI and [conjectured->measured] (leakage-bounded, P4) under estimated CSI (O2); the privacy floor under uncertain Eve CSI is chance-/worst-case-constrained (O3), not a free guarantee; the second-order Eve coupling is kept INSIDE the optimization (O4), never assumed zero. The method is physical-layer/scheduling-only (model weights untouched) and budget-conserving (energy-parity metric). Epistemic strength of Q5 == Q1: Q5 proves what Q1 calls provable and measures (with refutation triggers) what Q1 calls conjectured.

Epistemic-label ledger (method).

| Claim | Label |
|---|---|
| Joint noise+power allocation is non-convex; nullspace makes it block-separable under perfect-CSI + known-Eve + handled-second-order | [provable-by-construction] (structure) |
| Two-attractor conflict => no scalar feasible under heterogeneity (WATERLINE) | [provable-by-construction] |
| O2 leakage bound E[|sum_k e_k a_k|^2] ~ sigma_csi^2 * B_noise | [conjectured->measured] |
| O3 chance-/worst-case privacy floor formulation | [designed] -> [conjectured->measured] (realized violation rate) |
| O4 second-order Eve-signal coupling nonzero, kept in optimization | [conjectured->measured] |
| O5 hysteresis removes gate chatter | [designed] -> [conjectured->measured] |
| O6 pooled metric is blind to the tail; stratified metrics reveal it | [provable-by-construction] -> [conjectured->measured] |
| O7 per-adversary Renyi-DP accounting under correlated pairwise noise | [designed] -> [conjectured->measured] |
| Tail accuracy gain, epsilon-violation -> ~0, pooled parity, GIMBAL > Placebo | [conjectured->measured] |
| E_collapse <= 1e-6 at homogeneous identity (CI gate) | [provable-by-construction] |

Named real artifacts used: Yan et al. [10] secure-private OTA-FL (B1); COTAF / Sery et al. [6] analog aggregation (B2); broadband analog aggregation / truncated channel inversion, Zhu et al. [2] (B4, system model); pairwise-suppressible secure aggregation (B5); q-FFL / q-FedAvg, Li et al. [18] (B6); Goel-Negi artificial noise [22] (O3 robust design); Renyi-DP / moments accountant, Abadi et al. [14] (O7); SCA + alpha-fairness (solver/objective); MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, LEAF FEMNIST / Shakespeare; Dirichlet(alpha) non-IID partition (Hsu et al.); LeNet-5 / small CNN / ResNet-20 / 2-layer CNN / stacked LSTM models.

**Q5.9 -- Responsible-AI review (Rai): seven advisory folds integrated into Q5.**

Verdict: YELLOW -- proceed with seven advisory folds integrated into Q5. The proposal is a research idea (no shipped code, no production deployment); the privacy/fairness/dual-use concerns are real but standard for the domain and addressable with qualification language and added experiments; the exact-collapse gate is a positive safety feature. No finding rises to RED.

FOLD-1 Privacy-claim honesty [YELLOW]. State all DP/MSE-security guarantees as worst-case or in-expectation with explicit CSI-error assumptions (delta_h bound). Add a sensitivity-analysis subsection varying CSI error 0-20% and reporting realized vs nominal epsilon_k. Replace "provably private" with qualified language. Frame the eavesdropper-CSI guarantee over an uncertainty ball, not a point estimate.

FOLD-2 Fairness disaggregation + backfire check [YELLOW]. Report accuracy AND realized epsilon_k disaggregated by distance bin, data-volume bin, and their 3x3 joint grid. Add a fairness-backfire falsification experiment: hold epsilon_k fixed while varying LIFT to confirm inclusion gain is not bought by privacy relaxation. In limitations, note that data-volume proxies for I_k may still under-serve genuinely scarce-but-high-value data (e.g., rare classes).

FOLD-3 Dual-use and eavesdropper-model limitations [YELLOW]. Add a one-paragraph "Responsible Use" note: SHROUD is defensive, not offensive; intended for single-operator or cooperating-operator FL (e.g., private 5G, enterprise Wi-Fi), not adversarial RF jamming. Add a threat-model qualification: passive eavesdropper with bounded w_k uncertainty; active, MIMO-diversity, or compromised-aggregator adversaries exceed the model. Recommend layering with cryptographic secure aggregation [16] for production defense-in-depth.

FOLD-4 Energy / budget-parity check [YELLOW]. Add the formal sum-power constraint sum_k [P_k(LIFT) + P_k(SHROUD)] <= sum_k P_k(baseline) in the optimization. Report total TX power per round in results. Quantify SHROUD energy overhead as a percentage of baseline TX energy. If parity is a hard goal, add it as a Lagrange constraint and report the Pareto frontier (privacy gain vs energy overhead).

FOLD-5 Dataset provenance and simulation clarity [YELLOW]. Add a "Datasets and Simulation" subsection: per-dataset source, license, one-line provenance note. For FEMNIST: note NIST SD-19 origin and de-identification. State explicitly that all wireless channels are fully synthetic (no real RF captures). Confirm no real user PII in any evaluation split.

FOLD-6 Excluded-device visibility and participation equity [YELLOW]. Log and report the per-round exclusion set and reason (privacy floor too tight, channel too poor, power budget exhausted). Report per-device inclusion rate across rounds; flag devices below 50% inclusion over 100 rounds as functionally excluded. Propose (future work) a credit/prioritization or feedback mechanism for persistently excluded devices. Acknowledge residual near-far exclusion in limitations.

FOLD-7 Exact-collapse gate -- ENDORSE [GREEN]. Retain E_collapse <= 1e-6 as a mandatory CI regression gate (do-no-harm anchor). Extend it to cover homogeneous epsilon_k, I_k, and h_k simultaneously. Add a monotonicity regression check (more heterogeneity -> strictly higher LIFT/SHROUD deltas than baseline). Document in the paper as a correctness guarantee. (This is a rare, positive safety property: the method provably degrades to a known-good secure baseline rather than silently diverging.)

## 3. Novel Insights and Recommendations

Insight 1 -- The confound is the contribution (WATERLINE as a theorem, not a complaint). The central move converts "tune the secure budget better" into "add the axis the budget never had." The single global budget N is forced to be both a per-device privacy floor (N >= F_k) and a per-device inclusion ceiling (N <= C_k); feasibility requires max_k F_k <= min_k C_k, which is EMPTY under heterogeneity. This is an empty-intersection impossibility [provable-by-construction] -- it cannot be repaired by better tuning, scheduling, or post-processing, because channel-weighted post-processing only corrects the MEAN distortion AFTER aggregation and cannot reconstruct a per-device privacy-vs-inclusion choice already collapsed into one scalar at transmit time. Naming WATERLINE reframes five separate point-fixes (post-processing, scheduling, power control, pairwise masking, fair reweighting) as five shadows of one missing degree of freedom.

Insight 2 -- Aggregator-nullspace decoupling dissolves the global infeasibility into K per-device feasible constraints. The load-bearing mechanism is that SHROUD places the privacy noise in the AGGREGATOR nullspace (pairwise design with sum_k h_k a_k = 0), so it cancels in the computed sum while still degrading every other receiver (sum_k w_k a_k != 0). This makes the privacy noise FREE to the aggregate ([designed], exact under perfect CSI), which is precisely what breaks the privacy-vs-fidelity trade-off: the one infeasible global interval becomes K independently-feasible per-device constraints, with SHROUD owning the privacy floor and LIFT owning the inclusion ceiling. The two knobs are then orthogonal and independently ablatable by construction. Under estimated CSI the null is inexact and a residual leakage ~ sigma_csi^2 * B_noise re-enters the aggregate [conjectured->measured]; the design carries this as an explicit budgeted term rather than assuming it away.

Insight 3 -- A transferable pattern for analog-superposition secure computation. "Verify the privacy FLOOR and the inclusion CEILING JOINTLY, per device, not the pooled mean; then decouple them via the trusted-functional nullspace" is a general recipe for any over-the-air computation / multi-access secure-aggregation setting where a trusted linear functional must be preserved while untrusted observers are degraded [20], [13]. It applies to over-the-air secure aggregation, distributed estimation, and multi-access sensing beyond this one FL scheme. The companion methodological lesson is that the harm is invisible to pooled metrics, so stratified instruments (tail-device accuracy, per-device epsilon-violation rate, near-far inclusion rate) are mandatory to even detect it ([provable-by-construction] that pooled means are dominated by strong devices; [conjectured->measured] that stratified metrics reveal the gap).

Insight 4 -- An exact-collapse do-no-harm safety contract. GIMBAL is engineered so that at the homogeneous identity (epsilon_k = epsilon, |h_k| = h, I_k = I, gates = 1) it emits Yan et al.'s [10] uniform subset-Gaussian + channel-weighted post-processing allocation-for-allocation, with E_collapse <= 1e-6 enforced as a CI regression gate, and it is budget-conserving (it reallocates, never inflates, the total noise+power) [provable-by-construction]. This is a rare, machine-checkable safety property: the method can only help or exactly tie the named secure baseline, and any heterogeneous gain is a falsifiable empirical claim (refutation triggers R1-R3), never a guaranteed improvement.

Recommendations. (1) Report ALL privacy/inclusion metrics DISAGGREGATED by distance and data-volume bins, with the fairness-backfire check. (2) State every DP/MSE-security guarantee with explicit CSI-error and eavesdropper-uncertainty assumptions (worst-case or chance-constrained), never as an unqualified "provably private." (3) Enforce budget parity as a hard constraint and report total TX power and SHROUD energy overhead. (4) Keep the exact-collapse gate and add a monotonicity regression check. (5) Frame SHROUD as a defensive, single-operator mechanism and layer it with cryptographic secure aggregation [16] in production. (6) Log and report per-device inclusion rates so residual near-far exclusion is observable and contestable. These are elaborated in the RAI review folded into Q5.

Cited by author/title/venue/year only (foundational; no arXiv ID exists -- NOT fabricated):

Evidence and verification notes. Of the 25 references, 19 ([1]-[19]) were VERIFIED by fetching arxiv.org/abs/<id> and reading back the title character-for-character; arXiv IDs were NOT taken on faith from search snippets. The remaining 6 ([20]-[25]) are pre-arXiv or non-arXiv foundational works, cited by author/title/venue/year with NO fabricated identifier. Reference [10] is the source survey's reference 78 -- "Toward Secure and Private Over-the-Air Federated Learning," arXiv:2210.07669 (Yan, Wang, Zhi, Pan, Chai, Poor). A free-text search for the "pairwise-suppressible artificial noise" line returned two IEEE links with mismatched author/title pairs (a likely hallucination); they were REJECTED and are not cited. The pairwise-suppressible concept is instead grounded in verified prior art: cryptographic pairwise-mask secure aggregation [16] and physical-layer artificial noise [22], re-instantiated at the OTA physical layer (the SHROUD construction). Every reference [1]-[25] is cited at least once in Sections 1-3 above, and no citation refers to a number outside [1]-[25] (bidirectional integrity).
